fields.description:
## What
Add an `mcp_registry` job to `.github/workflows/release.yml` that publishes
`server.json` to the MCP Registry on a `v*` tag using **GitHub Actions OIDC**
(`mcp-publisher login github-oidc`) â€” eliminating the interactive
`mcp-publisher login github` device flow (whose cached Registry JWT expires and
was the one manual, friction-prone channel during the v0.7.0 release).

## Why
PyPI already publishes via OIDC Trusted Publishing and (change 314/316) GitHub
Releases are auto-created. The MCP Registry was the last channel still requiring a
human browser login. OIDC needs no stored secret: the runner's OIDC token proves the
`navapbc/rebar` repo identity, which authorizes the `io.github.navapbc/*` namespace.

## Implementation (per modelcontextprotocol.io/registry/github-actions)
- New job `mcp_registry`, `needs: publish` (runs after PyPI so the registry's
  package-ownership check resolves against the live version), same
  `if: github.event_name == 'push' && startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')` gate.
- `permissions: { id-token: write, contents: read }`.
- Steps: `actions/checkout@v5` (server.json lives in the repo) -> install mcp-publisher
  (curl the release binary, os/arch-detected) -> `./mcp-publisher login github-oidc`
  -> `./mcp-publisher publish`.
- No version step needed: the `Release X.Y.Z` commit bumps server.json before the tag,
  so the tag tree already carries the correct version.
- Update `docs/releasing.md` section 4 to mark MCP Registry as automated (OIDC in CI),
  demoting the manual `mcp-publisher login/publish` to a fallback.

## Verification note (end-to-end)
Like the sibling `publish` and `github_release` jobs, this job runs ONLY on a real
`v*` tag push and cannot execute in Gerrit branch CI (no tag, no OIDC token). So the
live end-to-end path (login github-oidc -> publish -> registry updated) is verified
on the NEXT tagged release (v0.7.1+), not at merge time. Pre-merge verification is
necessarily static: YAML well-formedness + job wiring, and that the install/login/
publish commands match the canonical upstream recipe. This deferral is intentional
and mirrors how the existing OIDC PyPI publish job is verified.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `.github/workflows/release.yml` contains an `mcp_registry` job gated to `v*` tag push with `permissions.id-token: write`.
- [ ] That job checks out the repo, installs mcp-publisher, runs `mcp-publisher login github-oidc`, then `mcp-publisher publish`.
- [ ] The job declares `needs: publish` so it runs after the PyPI publish.
- [ ] `docs/releasing.md` documents MCP Registry publishing as automated via OIDC, with the manual `mcp-publisher login github` path demoted to a fallback.
- [ ] The workflow YAML parses (validated with a YAML loader) and the commit lands on main through Gerrit (LLM-Review + Verified green).
- [ ] Live end-to-end confirmation is explicitly deferred to the next tagged release (v0.7.1+), per the Verification note above â€” not required at merge.
